title: Working at PwC helped me build technical skills quickly The name carries weight on a resume and I appreciated the formal training early on
pros: working at pwc helped me build technical skills quickly the name carries weight on a resume and i appreciated the formal training early on
cons: as an audit associate at pwc i experienced one of the most grueling work environments of my career the busy season is relentless you re expected to completely sacrifice your personal life for nearly four months out of the year late nights until 2 3 am are not uncommon and weekends are often treated as regular workdays what made the experience worse was the lack of support from senior team members and management rather than fostering a collaborative environment seniors and managers frequently overloaded associates with excessive tasks while providing minimal guidance when help was offered it often came with condescending attitudes dismissive remarks or outright belittling there was little to no regard for employee well-being despite putting in long hours and significant effort i was laid off after two years with little explanation or appreciation for the sacrifices i made overall my time at pwc left me burned out and disillusioned i would not recommend this role to anyone who values work-life balance a supportive team or long-term career growth
text: Working at PwC helped me build technical skills quickly The name carries weight on a resume and I appreciated the formal training early on working at pwc helped me build technical skills quickly the name carries weight on a resume and i appreciated the formal training early on as an audit associate at pwc i experienced one of the most grueling work environments of my career the busy season is relentless you re expected to completely sacrifice your personal life for nearly four months out of the year late nights until 2 3 am are not uncommon and weekends are often treated as regular workdays what made the experience worse was the lack of support from senior team members and management rather than fostering a collaborative environment seniors and managers frequently overloaded associates with excessive tasks while providing minimal guidance when help was offered it often came with condescending attitudes dismissive remarks or outright belittling there was little to no regard for employee well-being despite putting in long hours and significant effort i was laid off after two years with little explanation or appreciation for the sacrifices i made overall my time at pwc left me burned out and disillusioned i would not recommend this role to anyone who values work-life balance a supportive team or long-term career growth

title: In office policy is pretty relaxed most people are in 2 3 days per week on average in busy season it tends to go up and in less busy times people go in much less Firm benefits are amazing healthcare plans and 401k match are good time off is accrued but carries over YoY and paid out upon leaving if you have balance left Large start class if you begin out of college is great there are not many companies where you can start your career with hundreds of other kids in your same office in some cases so you get to meet a ton of people and build your network early Learning and development is a huge part of our job constant opportunities to grow your knowledge base in both your specific field as well as branch out to other topics The firm pays for CPA study materials from Becker along with one of each test There also is a CPA bonus which is amazing Opportunities to get involved in your community outside of work with different groups and organizations and we get paid time off to do community service which is wonderful A lot of firm sponsored happy hours and fun events are put on for your specific groups which allows you to meet co workers and partners in a relaxed atmosphere The holiday party and promotion party are amazing the firm goes all out and puts on amazing events The pay scale is extremely transparent which allows you to know exactly what you will be paid at the next levels as well as bonus percentages Bonuses are based on performance and you are placed into three different buckets to receive a percent of your salary
pros: - in office policy is pretty relaxed most people are in 2-3 days per week on average in busy season it tends to go up and in less busy times people go in much less - firm benefits are amazing healthcare plans and 401k match are good time off is accrued but carries over yoy and paid out upon leaving if you have balance left - large start class if you begin out of college is great there are not many companies where you can start your career with hundreds of other kids in your same office in some cases so you get to meet a ton of people and build your network early - learning and development is a huge part of our job constant opportunities to grow your knowledge base in both your specific field as well as branch out to other topics - the firm pays for cpa study materials from becker along with one of each test there also is a cpa bonus which is amazing - opportunities to get involved in your community outside of work with different groups and organizations and we get paid time off to do community service which is wonderful - a lot of firm sponsored happy hours and fun events are put on for your specific groups which allows you to meet co workers and partners in a relaxed atmosphere - the holiday party and promotion party are amazing the firm goes all out and puts on amazing events the pay scale is extremely transparent which allows you to know exactly what you will be paid at the next levels as well as bonus percentages bonuses are based on performance and you are placed into three different buckets to receive a percent of your salary
cons: - busy season hours aren't great it seems like we are slightly understaffed and it begins to show during our busiest months - some individuals are promoted to the next level a bit early not allowing them to develop fully which in turn has negative impacts on team productivity and work product - similar to every firm you will run into some managers and higher ups who micro-manage there is no way to review higher ups only to review people lower than you in the management structure so there is nobody giving feedback to these people over their management styles
